description: This figure presents the second-step machine learning-based performance screening for low-density FeMnAlC steels, illustrating composition optimization via contour maps and scatter plots. Panel (a) displays a contour map of predicted κ-carbide volume fraction (Y₄) as functions of aluminum (5.0–9.0 wt.%) and carbon (0.4–1.1 wt.%) content, with color intensity indicating volume fractions ranging from 0.45% to 13.15%. Panel (c) shows the corresponding density contour map (Υ₅), revealing values decreasing from 7.234 to 6.812 g/cm³ with increasing aluminum content. Panel (b) delineates the screening protocol where blue circles represent compositions meeting the κ fraction target (≥11.000%) and green circles represent density targets (≤6.900 g/cm³), with overlapping regions identifying 30 viable candidates satisfying dual constraints; the red star marks the experimentally validated composition Fe-30Mn-8Al-1C. This demonstrates the ML-guided optimization workflow for identifying lightweight, high-strength steel compositions.
